tools.name: users.gmailUsersDraftsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new draft with the `DRAFT` label.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersDraftsDelete
tools.description: Immediately and permanently deletes the specified draft. Does not simply trash it.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersDraftsGet
tools.description: Gets the specified draft.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersDraftsList
tools.description: Lists the drafts in the user's mailbox.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersDraftsSend
tools.description: Sends the specified, existing draft to the recipients in the `To`, `Cc`, and `Bcc` headers.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersDraftsUpdate
tools.description: Replaces a draft's content.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersGetProfile
tools.description: Gets the current user's Gmail profile.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersHistoryList
tools.description: Lists the history of all changes to the given mailbox. History results are returned in chronological order (increasing `historyId`).

tools.name: users.gmailUsersLabelsCreate
tools.description: Creates a new label.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersLabelsDelete
tools.description: Immediately and permanently deletes the specified label and removes it from any messages and threads that it is applied to.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersLabelsGet
tools.description: Gets the specified label.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersLabelsList
tools.description: Lists all labels in the user's mailbox.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersLabelsPatch
tools.description: Patch the specified label.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersLabelsUpdate
tools.description: Updates the specified label.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesAttachmentsGet
tools.description: Gets the specified message attachment.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesBatchDelete
tools.description: Deletes many messages by message ID. Provides no guarantees that messages were not already deleted or even existed at all.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesBatchModify
tools.description: Modifies the labels on the specified messages.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesDelete
tools.description: Immediately and permanently deletes the specified message. This operation cannot be undone. Prefer `messages.trash` instead.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesGet
tools.description: Gets the specified message.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesImport
tools.description: Imports a message into only this user's mailbox, with standard email delivery scanning and classification similar to receiving via SMTP. This method doesn't perform SPF checks, so it might not work for some spam messages, such as those attempting to perform domain spoofing. This method does not send a message. Note: This function doesn't trigger forwarding rules or filters set up by the user.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesInsert
tools.description: Directly inserts a message into only this user's mailbox similar to `IMAP APPEND`, bypassing most scanning and classification. Does not send a message.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesList
tools.description: Lists the messages in the user's mailbox.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesModify
tools.description: Modifies the labels on the specified message.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesSend
tools.description: Sends the specified message to the recipients in the `To`, `Cc`, and `Bcc` headers. For example usage, see [Sending email](https://developers.google.com/gmail/api/guides/sending).

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesTrash
tools.description: Moves the specified message to the trash.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersMessagesUntrash
tools.description: Removes the specified message from the trash.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseIdentitiesCreate
tools.description: Creates and configures a client-side encryption identity that's authorized to send mail from the user account. Google publishes the S/MIME certificate to a shared domain-wide directory so that people within a Google Workspace organization can encrypt and send mail to the identity.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseIdentitiesDelete
tools.description: Deletes a client-side encryption identity. The authenticated user can no longer use the identity to send encrypted messages. You cannot restore the identity after you delete it. Instead, use the CreateCseIdentity method to create another identity with the same configuration.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseIdentitiesGet
tools.description: Retrieves a client-side encryption identity configuration.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseIdentitiesList
tools.description: Lists the client-side encrypted identities for an authenticated user.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseIdentitiesPatch
tools.description: Associates a different key pair with an existing client-side encryption identity. The updated key pair must validate against Google's [S/MIME certificate profiles](https://support.google.com/a/answer/7300887).

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseKeypairsCreate
tools.description: Creates and uploads a client-side encryption S/MIME public key certificate chain and private key metadata for the authenticated user.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseKeypairsDisable
tools.description: Turns off a client-side encryption key pair. The authenticated user can no longer use the key pair to decrypt incoming CSE message texts or sign outgoing CSE mail. To regain access, use the EnableCseKeyPair to turn on the key pair. After 30 days, you can permanently delete the key pair by using the ObliterateCseKeyPair method.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseKeypairsEnable
tools.description: Turns on a client-side encryption key pair that was turned off. The key pair becomes active again for any associated client-side encryption identities.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseKeypairsGet
tools.description: Retrieves an existing client-side encryption key pair.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseKeypairsList
tools.description: Lists client-side encryption key pairs for an authenticated user.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsCseKeypairsObliterate
tools.description: Deletes a client-side encryption key pair permanently and immediately. You can only permanently delete key pairs that have been turned off for more than 30 days. To turn off a key pair, use the DisableCseKeyPair method. Gmail can't restore or decrypt any messages that were encrypted by an obliterated key. Authenticated users and Google Workspace administrators lose access to reading the encrypted messages.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsDelegatesCreate
tools.description: Adds a delegate with its verification status set directly to `accepted`, without sending any verification email. The delegate user must be a member of the same Google Workspace organization as the delegator user. Gmail imposes limitations on the number of delegates and delegators each user in a Google Workspace organization can have. These limits depend on your organization, but in general each user can have up to 25 delegates and up to 10 delegators. Note that a delegate user must be referred to by their primary email address, and not an email alias. Also note that when a new delegate is created, there may be up to a one minute delay before the new delegate is available for use. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsDelegatesDelete
tools.description: Removes the specified delegate (which can be of any verification status), and revokes any verification that may have been required for using it. Note that a delegate user must be referred to by their primary email address, and not an email alias. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsDelegatesGet
tools.description: Gets the specified delegate. Note that a delegate user must be referred to by their primary email address, and not an email alias. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsDelegatesList
tools.description: Lists the delegates for the specified account. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsFiltersCreate
tools.description: Creates a filter. Note: you can only create a maximum of 1,000 filters.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsFiltersDelete
tools.description: Immediately and permanently deletes the specified filter.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsFiltersGet
tools.description: Gets a filter.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsFiltersList
tools.description: Lists the message filters of a Gmail user.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsForwardingAddressesCreate
tools.description: Creates a forwarding address. If ownership verification is required, a message will be sent to the recipient and the resource's verification status will be set to `pending`; otherwise, the resource will be created with verification status set to `accepted`. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsForwardingAddressesDelete
tools.description: Deletes the specified forwarding address and revokes any verification that may have been required. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsForwardingAddressesGet
tools.description: Gets the specified forwarding address.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsForwardingAddressesList
tools.description: Lists the forwarding addresses for the specified account.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsGetAutoForwarding
tools.description: Gets the auto-forwarding setting for the specified account.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsGetImap
tools.description: Gets IMAP settings.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsGetLanguage
tools.description: Gets language settings.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsGetPop
tools.description: Gets POP settings.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsGetVacation
tools.description: Gets vacation responder settings.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsCreate
tools.description: Creates a custom "from" send-as alias. If an SMTP MSA is specified, Gmail will attempt to connect to the SMTP service to validate the configuration before creating the alias. If ownership verification is required for the alias, a message will be sent to the email address and the resource's verification status will be set to `pending`; otherwise, the resource will be created with verification status set to `accepted`. If a signature is provided, Gmail will sanitize the HTML before saving it with the alias. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsDelete
tools.description: Deletes the specified send-as alias. Revokes any verification that may have been required for using it. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsGet
tools.description: Gets the specified send-as alias. Fails with an HTTP 404 error if the specified address is not a member of the collection.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsList
tools.description: Lists the send-as aliases for the specified account. The result includes the primary send-as address associated with the account as well as any custom "from" aliases.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsPatch
tools.description: Patch the specified send-as alias.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsSmimeInfoDelete
tools.description: Deletes the specified S/MIME config for the specified send-as alias.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsSmimeInfoGet
tools.description: Gets the specified S/MIME config for the specified send-as alias.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsSmimeInfoInsert
tools.description: Insert (upload) the given S/MIME config for the specified send-as alias. Note that pkcs12 format is required for the key.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsSmimeInfoList
tools.description: Lists S/MIME configs for the specified send-as alias.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsSmimeInfoSetDefault
tools.description: Sets the default S/MIME config for the specified send-as alias.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsUpdate
tools.description: Updates a send-as alias. If a signature is provided, Gmail will sanitize the HTML before saving it with the alias. Addresses other than the primary address for the account can only be updated by service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsSendAsVerify
tools.description: Sends a verification email to the specified send-as alias address. The verification status must be `pending`. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsUpdateAutoForwarding
tools.description: Updates the auto-forwarding setting for the specified account. A verified forwarding address must be specified when auto-forwarding is enabled. This method is only available to service account clients that have been delegated domain-wide authority.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsUpdateImap
tools.description: Updates IMAP settings.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsUpdateLanguage
tools.description: Updates language settings. If successful, the return object contains the `displayLanguage` that was saved for the user, which may differ from the value passed into the request. This is because the requested `displayLanguage` may not be directly supported by Gmail but have a close variant that is, and so the variant may be chosen and saved instead.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsUpdatePop
tools.description: Updates POP settings.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersSettingsUpdateVacation
tools.description: Updates vacation responder settings.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersStop
tools.description: Stop receiving push notifications for the given user mailbox.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersThreadsDelete
tools.description: Immediately and permanently deletes the specified thread. Any messages that belong to the thread are also deleted. This operation cannot be undone. Prefer `threads.trash` instead.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersThreadsGet
tools.description: Gets the specified thread.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersThreadsList
tools.description: Lists the threads in the user's mailbox.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersThreadsModify
tools.description: Modifies the labels applied to the thread. This applies to all messages in the thread.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersThreadsTrash
tools.description: Moves the specified thread to the trash. Any messages that belong to the thread are also moved to the trash.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersThreadsUntrash
tools.description: Removes the specified thread from the trash. Any messages that belong to the thread are also removed from the trash.

tools.name: users.gmailUsersWatch
tools.description: Set up or update a push notification watch on the given user mailbox.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
